notes: Eval 1 old_skill got lucky: agent picked style named 'Structured Sidebar Scholar' and inferred sidebar from the name. This is NOT reliable — eval 3 old_skill with 'Terminal Scholar' (same visual register) got single-column.
Key discriminating assertion: 'Q2 options include layout descriptor' — passes 100% new, 0% old. Clean signal.
Old skill evals 2 and 3 produce structurally identical single-column HTML despite different Q2 choices — no layout diversity mechanism existed.
New skill evals 1 and 2 produce different layouts from different Q2 choices (sticky sidebar vs. single column) — layout diversity now works deterministically.
Time overhead: new skill ~67s longer average — expected; additional layout spec in design doc and impl plan adds depth.
